prompt: I started a small candle-making business in June 2024 while working my day job (£42,000 salary). I put in £8,000 of my own savings to buy equipment, supplies, and set up a website. I've only made about £1,400 in sales so far. I work on it evenings and weekends from my spare bedroom in my rented flat. My partner says I should just write it off on my taxes but I'm not sure how that works. Can the money I put in count as a business expense? Can I get tax back on the loss?

assertions.text: Distinguishes capital expenses (equipment) from revenue expenses (supplies, website costs)
Mentions capital allowances (e.g. Annual Investment Allowance) for equipment
Explains sideways loss relief to offset loss against employment income
Mentions early years relief as an option for a new business
Addresses WFH costs (flat rate or actual proportional costs for spare bedroom)
Warns about hobby vs trade test — business must be commercial to claim relief
Provides a realistic estimate of potential tax saving from the loss
